channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: From Sayonara Wild Hearts to Stray, Annapurna brings 5 games to Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Annapurna Interactive is embracing the Nintendo Switch 2, announcing ports and upgrades for a fistful of their back catalogue of games. This is starting with Sayonara Wild Hearts and Lorelei and the Laser Eyes, and will continue through to cyberpunk kitty adventure Stray.

The five games include:

  • Lorelei and the Laser Eyes (available now)
  • Sayonara Wild Hearts (available now)
  • Stray (28th May)
  • to a T
  • Wanderstop

Now, some of these are already on Switch, and so are getting upgrade. Sayonara and Lorelai both have free upgrades for existing Switch version owners, bringing support for 120Hz and 4K support. Sayonara Wild Hearts also includes the Remix Arcade mode, ramping up the speed of the gameplay as you aim for high scores.

Stray’s upgrade will arrive in May, also coming with improved visuals, 4K and higher frame rate, and even mouse control support.

Then in the summer, to a T will arrive on Switch 2 on 11th June, while Wanderstop will arrive on 23rd June across both Switch and Switch 2.

This is all in addition to People of Note already being out on Switch 2, and Mixtape, which will arrive on 7th May.

A busy time all round for Annapurna, but great news for Switch 2 owners.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/23/from-sayonara-wild-hearts-to-stray-annapurna-brings-5-games-to-switch-2/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Deep Dish Dungeon brings foodie dungeon crawling to Xbox, PC & Game Pass this autumn
item.content:encoded:
Behold Studios and publisher Raw Fury have announced that their dungeon crawler, Deep Dish Dungeon will be coming to Xbox Series X|S, PC and Game Pass on day one this autumn, putting a culinary spin on the genre.

Deep Dish Dungeon will see you explore an underground maze, either solo or in a group of up to three. As you go, you’ll have to forage and scavenge for food to keep your tummies full, starting off with simply roasting some apples or mushrooms, going through making hearty stews, and building up to cooking the most powerful meal in the game: the deep dish pizza.

This is a hand-crafted dungeon, as opposed to a generated roguelite-y one, and Behold Studio has filled it with many secrets. There’s puzzles, there’s enemies to fight, and there’s lots of symbols and strange things to find and figure out what they mean, gradually unravelling some mystery at the heart of the place.

It’s all rendered in a pretty nice mid-to-late 90s 3D style, with chunky polygonal characters, simplistic animations, and blotchy texture work.

The game is all-in on the Xbox and PC ecosystem, so in addition to launching on those platforms and Game Pass, it will support Xbox Play Anywhere and, as is to be expected, it will be Xbox handheld certified.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/24/deep-dish-dungeon-brings-foodie-dungeon-crawling-to-xbox-pc-game-pass-this-autumn/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Here’s your first look at the Elden Ring movie
item.content:encoded:
Filming is underway for Alex Garland’s Elden Ring movie and I think this leaked set video was filmed at the Royal Naval College in Greenwich, London. In it we can see a Dung Eater getting pelted by an angry mob.

We also have a short video where you can just about make out actor Kit Connor.

Bandai Namco Entertainment and movie production studio A24 have teamed up with Alex Garland to make the Elden Ring, with the release date set for March 3, 2028.

The cast list is impressive with some big names signed up already, but we do not know what characters they will be playing.

  • Kit Connor (Warfare, Heartstopper)
  • Ben Whishaw (Skyfall, Paddington)
  • Cailee Spaeny (Alien: Romulus, Civil War)
  • Tom Burke (Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga, Black Bag)
  • Havana Rose Liu (Bottoms)
  • Sonoya Mizuno (Ex Machina)
  • Jonathan Pryce (The Two Popes)
  • Ruby Cruz (Willow, Bottoms)
  • Nick Offerman (The Last of Us)
  • John Hodgkinson
  • Jefferson Hall
  • Emma Laird
  • Peter Serafinowicz

Alex Garland has both written and will direct the film. Garland is a well-respected movie producer and reportedly pitched the idea himself to Bandai Namco. He’s also a big gamer with many highly regarded movies under his belt, including Civil War (2024), Sunshine (2007), Ex Machina (2014) and Annihilation (2018).

According to The Hollywood Reporter, the film adaptation is set to have a budget well over $100 million, with around 100 days of principal photography planned, making it the most expensive film A24 has worked on.

News of the movie adaptation was announced over a year ago but things have been very quiet, but apparently moving at quite a pace.

Elden Ring was created under the guidance of FromSoftware’s Hidetaka Miyazaki, based on a mythological story written by George R. R. Martin, author of the fantasy novel series, “A Song of Ice and Fire.” The game has sold an astonishing 30 million copies worldwide as of April 2025, and spawned a new entry in the series with Elden Ring: Nightreign, which launched on 30th of May 2025.

Source: YouTube



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/24/heres-your-first-look-at-the-elden-ring-movie/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
